Choose exactly one action for the current Grid2Op state.
Return only a valid GridAction JSON object with keys: line_set, redispatch, do_nothing.

single_fault_objective=bring max_rho below 0.80; choose redispatch while above target if it lowers max_rho; choose do_nothing mainly after target is met.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"primary_action":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_1":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},"backup_action_2":{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: For single_fault, do not propose disconnect_line or reconnect_line. Use redispatch and do_nothing only. Solve congestion by shifting generation, not by cutting topology.
TASK RULE: Rank your output strictly as primary_action first, then backup_action_1, then backup_action_2. The simulator will test all three and execute the highest-ranked safe option.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[161]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=5
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[{"line_id":125,"rho":1.1481}]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":162,"rho":0.8511}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

Do not include markdown, prose, reasoning, or extra keys.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[125,161]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=4
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,124,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":162,"rho":0.819}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

messages.content:
You are a power-grid control policy.
Choose exactly one action for the current Grid2Op state.
Return only a valid GridAction JSON object with keys: line_set, redispatch, do_nothing.
Do not include markdown, prose, reasoning, or extra keys.
task_id=n_minus_1
step=3/20
n_minus_1_objective=score rewards early emergency clearing and steady security, not only immediate simulated_reward.
n_minus_1_thresholds=steps 1-5 should reduce max_rho below 0.92; steps 6-20 should keep max_rho below 0.90.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,125,161]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=3
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,124,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,125,161]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=2
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,124,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.
task_id=n_minus_1
step=4/20
max_rho=0.663
line_count=186

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[12]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=5
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,12]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=4
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

messages.content:
You are a power-grid control policy.
Choose exactly one action for the current Grid2Op state.
Return only a valid GridAction JSON object with keys: line_set, redispatch, do_nothing.
Do not include markdown, prose, reasoning, or extra keys.
task_id=n_minus_1
step=3/20
n_minus_1_objective=score rewards early emergency clearing and steady security, not only immediate simulated_reward.
n_minus_1_thresholds=steps 1-5 should reduce max_rho below 0.92; steps 6-20 should keep max_rho below 0.90.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,12]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=3
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,12]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=2
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,117]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=4
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":88,"rho":0.8525},{"line_id":154,"rho":0.8466},{"line_id":93,"rho":0.8164}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

messages.content:
You are a power-grid control policy.
Choose exactly one action for the current Grid2Op state.
Return only a valid GridAction JSON object with keys: line_set, redispatch, do_nothing.
Do not include markdown, prose, reasoning, or extra keys.
task_id=n_minus_1
step=3/20
n_minus_1_objective=score rewards early emergency clearing and steady security, not only immediate simulated_reward.
n_minus_1_thresholds=steps 1-5 should reduce max_rho below 0.92; steps 6-20 should keep max_rho below 0.90.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,117]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=3
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":154,"rho":0.8284},{"line_id":93,"rho":0.8092},{"line_id":88,"rho":0.8046}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINES=[]; disconnected_now=[2,117]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=2
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: When any faulted line cooldown reaches 0, include reconnect_line candidates for those lines unless graph intelligence or current overloads strongly suggest they are unsafe.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least one redispatch candidate aimed at immediate rho reduction. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest safe redispatch or a safe reconnect test rather than defaulting all candidates toward do_nothing.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[26,129]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.
task_id=n_minus_1
step=4/20
max_rho=0.792
line_count=186

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.

Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.

CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.

Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In cascade_prevent, prioritize lines with active overflow countdowns. A line with timestep_overflow=2 is more urgent than a line with high rho but overflow=0.
CASCADE RULE: Prevent automatic trips first, then improve thermal margin. Triaging imminent countdown expirations is more important than slightly reducing global max_rho.
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[{"line_id":169,"rho":1.0361,"timestep_overflow":2},{"line_id":170,"rho":1.028,"timestep_overflow":2}]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.
CONTROLLED_ISLANDING_CANDIDATES=[{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":2,"expected_rho_change":-0.078246,"guidance_priority_score":2.077511,"available_load_ratio":0.934577,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":162,"expected_rho_change":-0.07813,"guidance_priority_score":2.077279,"available_load_ratio":0.934577,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":46,"expected_rho_change":-0.070089,"guidance_priority_score":1.961198,"available_load_ratio":0.934577,"available_island_ratio":1.0}]
REDISPATCH_CANDIDATES=[]

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.
CONTROLLED_ISLANDING_CANDIDATES=[{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":174,"expected_rho_change":-0.182237,"guidance_priority_score":2.477028,"available_load_ratio":0.932977,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":106,"expected_rho_change":-0.086926,"guidance_priority_score":2.286407,"available_load_ratio":0.932977,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":105,"expected_rho_change":-0.028913,"guidance_priority_score":2.070381,"available_load_ratio":0.932977,"available_island_ratio":1.0}]
REDISPATCH_CANDIDATES=[]
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[{"line_id":106,"rho":1.0438,"timestep_overflow":1}]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.
CONTROLLED_ISLANDING_CANDIDATES=[]
REDISPATCH_CANDIDATES=[{"action_type":"redispatch","target_id":42,"delta_mw":11.2,"expected_rho_change":-0.079903,"guidance_priority_score":2.256659,"available_load_ratio":0.925868,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"redispatch","target_id":45,"delta_mw":11.2,"expected_rho_change":-0.079899,"guidance_priority_score":2.256652,"available_load_ratio":0.925868,"available_island_ratio":1.0},{"action_type":"redispatch","target_id":42,"delta_mw":5.6,"expected_rho_change":-0.079816,"guidance_priority_score":2.256486,"available_load_ratio":0.925868,"available_island_ratio":1.0}]
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.
CONTROLLED_ISLANDING_CANDIDATES=[]
REDISPATCH_CANDIDATES=[]
OVERFLOW_COUNTDOWNS=[]
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.

TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.

BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false

TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
Propose exactly 3 candidate actions to test in the physics sandbox.
Allowed action types: disconnect_line, reconnect_line, redispatch, do_nothing.
Return a single JSON object only.
Use this exact schema: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"},{"action_type":"disconnect_line|reconnect_line|redispatch|do_nothing","line_id":null|int,"gen_id":null|int,"delta_mw":null|float,"reason":"short string"}]}
Rules: no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
TASK RULE: In multi_stage_cascade, assume the collapse will continue across three stages. Do not optimize only for this step; position the grid so later stages keep more load available.
STAGE_CONTEXT=stage_1_of_3; steps_to_stage_boundary=0; available_load_ratio=1.0000; available_island_ratio=1.0000
BOUNDARY_STATUS=assessed:false; majority_islands_available:false
MSCF RULE: Prefer actions that preserve transferable generation and keep islands self-sustaining at the next boundary. Avoid short-term fixes that strand load in islands with insufficient generation.
TASK RULE: With multiple overloaded lines, topology cuts risk bus isolation. Prioritize redispatch over disconnect_line unless the line is explicitly safe_to_disconnect and the action preserves connectivity.
CONTROLLED_ISLANDING_CANDIDATES=[{"action_type":"disconnect_line","target_id":108,"expected_rho_change":-0.001982,"guidance_priority_score":1.731611,"available_load_ratio":0.934911,"available_island_ratio":1.0}]

Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: You must prioritize candidates from the sensitivity_guidance list. These actions have been mathematically verified by power-flow sensitivity factors to reduce the load on the stressed line.
